metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific Gerrit endpoint + HTTP method the bot uses to remove another account's `Autosubmit` vote (e.g. `DELETE /a/changes/{id}/reviewers/{accountId}/votes/Autosubmit` or `POST /revisions/current/review` with `{"labels":{"Autosubmit":0}}`), confirm the bot's service account holds the `Remove Reviewer` ACL (or is an Administrator) in the S5 preconditions, and add that operation to the autolander Gerrit helper.
findings.finding: The hand-back AC says 'remove Autosubmit from every stack member' but names no Gerrit endpoint for vote removal, and no such capability exists in the codebase. The existing client only casts votes for the bot's own label (LLM-Review). Removing a vote originally cast by a *different* account (the contributing agent set Autosubmit+1; the autolander bot must zero it) requires either (a) POST /revisions/{rev}/review with {"labels": {"Autosubmit": 0}} — which Gerrit allows only for the same account or for an account with the 'Remove Reviewer' permission — or (b) DELETE /a/changes/{id}/reviewers/{accountId}/votes/{labelId}. Neither endpoint is named, neither is in the codebase, and the cross-account ACL precondition is absent.
findings.location: AC: Hand-back — 'remove Autosubmit from every stack member'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Hand-back AC, specify: 'zero the Autosubmit vote by calling `DELETE /a/changes/{id}/reviewers/{accountId}/votes/Autosubmit` for each stack member (requires the bot account to hold the `removeReviewer` ACL or be an Administrator — add to S5's provisioning checklist).' Add a corresponding helper method to `infra/autolander/gerrit.py` and cover the HTTP 403 error path in the hand-back unit tests.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC3, specify the Gerrit HTTP response discriminator for a 'not fast-forward' submit refusal (e.g., HTTP 409 with body containing `"not fast-forward"` or a specific Gerrit error code) so it can be distinguished from a rebase-conflict 409 (S3's domain) and be reliably unit-tested.
findings.finding: AC3 states a `not fast-forward` submit refusal 'is handled identically' to a TOCTOU pre-submit mismatch (triggering re-drive), but never specifies how the bot detects this condition from Gerrit's HTTP response — no HTTP status code, response body pattern, or error-message string is named.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'A `not fast-forward` refusal is identified by a `POST /submit` HTTP 409 whose response body contains the string `"not fast-forward"`; any other 409 is treated as a hard error and routes to S3's conflict path.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a state-transition table co-located with the `wipChain` schema in AC1, listing each (from_phase, trigger/guard, to_phase) tuple so AC5's unit tests have an authoritative FSM to validate against.
findings.finding: The phase enum is defined (AC1) but the full state-transition table is never co-located with the schema. Only one transition is specified inline (AC3: `phase→rebasing` on TOCTOU failure). Transitions such as `idle→selecting`, `selecting→rebasing`, `rebasing→awaiting_verified`, `awaiting_verified→submitting`, `submitting→idle` are implied but not stated. An implementer must infer the full FSM from scattered narrative.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a compact table to AC1, e.g.:
| From | Trigger | To |
|---|---|---|
| idle | front Autosubmit+submittable change found | selecting |
| selecting | rebase dispatched | rebasing |
| rebasing | all patchsets minted | awaiting_verified |
| awaiting_verified | all members Verified+1 on post-rebase SHA | submitting |
| submitting | POST /submit success | idle |
| submitting | TOCTOU mismatch / not-fast-forward AND re_drive_count < 5 | rebasing |
| submitting | TOCTOU mismatch / not-fast-forward AND re_drive_count == 5 | idle (after hand-back) |
| any | sentinel file present | paused |
| paused | sentinel file removed | idle |

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC3 (a) that `re_drive_count` increments at the START of each re-drive cycle (before the rebase call), (b) that a Gerrit API error during re-drive still consumes a slot (or explicitly does not), and (c) that `re_drive_count` is initialized to 0 when a new wipChain is selected.
findings.finding: `re_drive_count` increment placement is ambiguous: AC3 says 'at most N=5 times' and names the field, but never specifies (a) when exactly the counter is incremented (before vs. after the re-drive attempt), (b) whether a failed Gerrit rebase call within a re-drive cycle counts, or (c) whether the counter resets to 0 when a new wipChain is selected.
findings.location: AC3 (FFO TOCTOU guard + bounded re-drive), AC1 (`wipChain` schema)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC4, reproduce the marker field list from S3 and specify which `patchset_SHA` value S2b writes after N=5 re-drives (e.g., 'the SHA of the last rebased patchset for that member, as stored in `tested_shas`').
findings.finding: AC4 instructs the implementer to 'write S3's `needs_rebase` marker' but does not specify which fields S2b is responsible for populating, nor which `patchset_SHA` value to record after N=5 re-drives (the SHA of the last rebased patchset? the original patchset before any re-drive?). The marker schema lives in S3's ticket and is not reproduced here.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the hand-back sequence as idempotent (e.g., write the S3 marker first, then remove labels, then post comment) and specify retry semantics so a re-run of any step is safe.
findings.finding: The hand-back sequence (remove Autosubmit from every stack member + post comment + write needs_rebase marker) is a multi-step write with no stated idempotency or partial-failure recovery. A crash or Gerrit error after step 1 (label removal) but before step 3 (marker write) leaves S3/consumers without the marker, so the change appears handed-back to the author but the `land` loop may not recognize it.
findings.suggested_fix: Write the needs_rebase marker first (idempotent S3 put); then remove labels (Gerrit label set is idempotent for same value); then post comment with a dedup key (e.g., marker SHA or change+reason). On restart, check for existing marker before re-executing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the failure/partial-failure handling for the hand-back step: define retry policy and/or a fallback state (e.g., `phase→paused` + operator alert) when Gerrit label-removal or comment POST returns an error, so the state machine never silently stalls.
findings.finding: The hand-back procedure (label removal + Gerrit comment + marker write) specifies the happy path of that operation but does not address failure states: if the Gerrit label-removal API call or the comment POST fails (network error, permission error, Gerrit 409), the plan gives no guidance on retry, partial-failure handling, or how the state machine should recover without leaving the wipChain stuck in a non-idle phase.
findings.location: AC: Hand-back (prior art: Chromium CQ reset / Zuul dequeue-and-comment)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the hand-back AC: 'If any Gerrit API call in the hand-back sequence fails after up to M retries (e.g., M=3 with exponential back-off), transition `phase→paused`, emit an operator alert, and halt further processing; do not partially advance past the failed step.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit unit-test scenario in AC5 for `POST /submit` returning a `not fast-forward` refusal (as distinct from the pre-submit TOCTOU check failing), verifying that `re_drive_count` increments and phase transitions to `rebasing` (or hand-back fires after N=5 refusals on this path).
findings.finding: AC5 (Tests) does not explicitly call out a test for the `not fast-forward` Gerrit submit-refusal path, even though AC3 specifies it is handled identically to a TOCTOU mismatch (re-drive triggered). This is a new failure path on a new user-facing flow that the test plan leaves uncovered.
findings.location: AC5 (Tests) / AC3 (FFO TOCTOU guard + bounded re-drive)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Tests AC, append: '…the `not fast-forward` Gerrit submit-refusal (POST /submit returns error → re-drive triggered, re_drive_count incremented, hand-back after N=5 refusals on this path).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that S3's plan explicitly defines the `needs_rebase` marker (storage location, format, read/write contract) and that S2b's write aligns with that definition — if S3 has not yet defined it, define the interface here or create a shared spec before implementing.
findings.finding: The plan treats 'S3's `needs_rebase` marker' as pre-existing infrastructure that this story writes to ('write S3's `needs_rebase` marker' / 'Same mechanism S3 uses for conflict/CI-fail'), but a repo-wide grep finds zero occurrences of `needs_rebase` anywhere — no ADR, no code, no plan defines this marker. If S3 has not yet defined the marker schema (location, format, write/read contract), this story is writing to an undefined interface.
findings.location: AC — Hand-back: '+ write S3's `needs_rebase` marker' / '(Same mechanism S3 uses for conflict/CI-fail.)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that exercises the FFO TOCTOU guard end-to-end against a real or staging Gerrit instance (or document a rationale for deferring it to a named child ticket), so the live `POST /submit` path is covered by the plan's verification.
findings.finding: The Tests AC ('unit-test each phase transition, the TOCTOU guard, the bounded re-drive, and the hand-back') names only unit tests, which will mock the Gerrit `POST /submit` call. No acceptance criterion exercises the FFO TOCTOU guard end-to-end against a real or staging Gerrit instance, so the actual live submit path is never proven by the plan's verification strategy.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'Integration smoke-test (against staging Gerrit): seed a change whose tested SHA is stale (main advanced by one commit); confirm the lander detects the mismatch, increments re_drive_count, and does NOT call POST /submit; run with `pytest infra/autolander/tests/integration/ -k toctou_guard --gerrit-url $STAGING_GERRIT_URL`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to the Tests AC (e.g. `pytest infra/autolander/tests/unit/test_wip_chain.py -v` with an assertion that all four named scenario groups contain at least one test) so test completeness is mechanically verifiable.
findings.finding: The Tests AC lacks a concrete proving command, so there is no stated way to confirm the unit tests are green and all named scenarios are covered; 'unit-test each X' is effort-framed rather than evidence-producing.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Tests AC: 'Verified by: `pytest infra/autolander/tests/unit/test_wip_chain.py -v` — all four groups (phase-transitions, TOCTOU guard, bounded re-drive, hand-back) must contain ≥1 passing test case; CI must be green.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either convert AC bullet 2 into a concrete behavioral requirement for `loop.py` (e.g., 'the TOCTOU guard MUST call `submittable` re-check rather than infer tip-status independently') with a corresponding test, or move it to a 'Background / Context' section and remove the checkbox.
findings.finding: The second AC bullet ('`submittable` under FFO') describes Gerrit's native `submittable` semantics as background context but does not specify any verifiable deliverable or behavior in `loop.py` that an implementer must build or that a test must exercise. The Tests AC does not include a test for correct interpretation of `submittable`. This makes the bullet an orphan criterion — a checkbox with nothing to verify.
findings.suggested_fix: Move bullet 2 to a non-AC 'Context' block under '## Why' (e.g., 'Note: Gerrit's `submittable` flag already encodes on-tip + votes-at-MAX per ADR-0040; the TOCTOU guard in bullet 3 exploits this.'). Add a test case in AC5: 'TOCTOU guard uses `submittable=false` as the fast path and augments with explicit parent-SHA check to distinguish behind-tip from vote-fail.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Standardize on one term throughout all AC bullets and the plan body — either 'chain' (matching `wipChain` / `chain_member_ids`) or 'stack' (matching the Gerrit stacking metaphor) — and apply it consistently.
findings.finding: The plan uses 'chain' and 'stack' interchangeably for the same concept (the ordered set of related changes), creating terminology drift. The wipChain schema uses 'chain_member_ids', the TOCTOU AC uses 'chain' and 'chain member', but the hand-back AC switches to 'stack member' and 'stack'. The prior-art note also uses 'stack'. This drift means an implementer cannot tell whether 'stack' and 'chain' are the same abstraction.
findings.location: AC bullet 1 ('chain_member_ids[]'), AC bullet 3 ('for a chain: the bottom-most member'), AC bullet 4 ('every stack member', 'rebase onto current main'), Tests AC ('for a chain')
findings.suggested_fix: Use 'chain member' everywhere to match `chain_member_ids[]` and `wipChain`. Replace 'every stack member' in AC4 with 'every chain member', and update the user-facing comment text accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify retry/backoff (and a timeout) for each Gerrit API call in the state machine (TOCTOU re-confirm, POST /submit, rebase, label removal, comment post), and define what phase transition occurs on exhausted retries.
findings.finding: No retry/backoff or error handling is specified for any Gerrit API calls within the state machine (TOCTOU re-confirm, POST /submit, rebase, label-removal, comment-posting). A transient network error or Gerrit 5xx on any of these calls would leave wipChain stuck in its current phase with no recovery path defined.
findings.location: AC: FFO TOCTOU guard + bounded re-drive; AC: Hand-back
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `gerrit_call_with_retry(fn, max_attempts=3, backoff_base=2s)` wrapper; on exhausted retries transition wipChain to `idle` (or a new `error` sub-state) and emit a metric/alert.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add observability requirements: emit a structured log entry on every phase transition, a counter metric for TOCTOU guard fires and re-drive exhaustions, and an alert when wipChain remains non-idle beyond a configurable threshold.
findings.finding: No observability is specified for the state machine: no metrics for phase transitions, no counter for TOCTOU guard fires, no alert when re_drive_count hits N=5, and no structured log for Gerrit API failures. Operators cannot distinguish a stuck wipChain from a healthy one in steady state.
findings.location: AC: FFO TOCTOU guard + bounded re-drive; AC: wipChain schema

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the wipChain CREATE lifecycle: specify its initial value on process start, whether it is reconstructed from durable state (e.g., S3 marker + Gerrit label scan) after a restart, and how an in-progress `submitting` phase is safely resolved on recovery.
findings.finding: The wipChain creation lifecycle is undefined: the plan specifies the schema and phases but not when/how wipChain is initialized (e.g., on process start, on first change selection, or recovered from durable storage after a restart).
findings.suggested_fix: On startup, scan Gerrit for changes with Autosubmit+Verified and any existing needs_rebase markers to reconstruct wipChain; if a change is in `submitting`, treat it as needing TOCTOU re-confirmation before proceeding.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concurrency model for wipChain mutations: either declare the loop single-threaded/serial (one coroutine at a time via asyncio event loop with no yielding between check and act), or introduce an explicit asyncio.Lock around all phase transitions and the TOCTOU check-then-submit sequence.
findings.finding: Phase transition atomicity and concurrency safety of wipChain mutation are unspecified. If the event loop is async (I/O-bound loop strongly suggests it), two concurrent coroutines (e.g., a Gerrit webhook handler and the main loop tick) could interleave phase reads and writes without an explicit serialization mechanism.
findings.location: AC: wipChain schema; AC: FFO TOCTOU guard + bounded re-drive
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the TOCTOU-check → phase-set → POST /submit sequence in a single non-yielding asyncio critical section (no `await` between re-confirm read and phase mutation), or hold an asyncio.Lock for the duration.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that S4's `land` command spec explicitly defines label-removal (Autosubmit withdrawn by the bot) as the detectable hand-back signal, and cross-reference that spec here — or define the signal contract in a shared interface document both S2b and S4 reference.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Label-removal is the "handed back" signal `land` reads' as if `land`'s behaviour is a settled, verifiable contract. No `land` command exists anywhere in the repo (grep for 'land command', 'S4 land', 'land reads' all return zero codebase hits). This is a forward reference to S4's unbuilt agent command being treated as a defined dependency in S2b's hand-back AC.
findings.location: AC — Hand-back: 'Label-removal is the "handed back" signal `land` reads.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note to the Files section acknowledging that `infra/autolander/loop.py` is also edited by sibling tasks S2a and S2c, and clarify which logical section(s) of the file S2b owns (state-machine class, TOCTOU guard function, hand-back function) to prevent integration conflicts.
findings.finding: S2a (89f9-b381-60e2-4802) and S2c (1ccf-ca1e-f832-452e) are sibling tasks that also name `infra/autolander/loop.py` as their edit target. The plan does not acknowledge this shared-file surface, creating an unenumerated coordination dependency: whoever implements S2b must either coordinate merges with S2a/S2c authors or the plan must clarify the ownership boundary (e.g., S2b owns only the state-machine + guard + hand-back sections of loop.py, not the full file).
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Files section: '`infra/autolander/loop.py` — S2b section: the `WipChain` dataclass, phase-transition logic, `toctou_guard()`, and `hand_back()` function. (Sibling S2a owns selection/rebase-routing; S2c owns fresh-Verified await and submit call.)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate S4's `land` command (and its expected file path, e.g. `infra/autolander/land.py` or `src/rebar/...`) as an external consumer in the Files section, or add a cross-reference note so the hand-back contract is treated as a shared interface.
findings.finding: The hand-back AC says 'Label-removal is the handed-back signal `land` reads' — naming `land` (S4's agent command) as a consumer of the hand-back output — but `land` is not listed in the Files section as an affected consumer. If `land` polls for `Autosubmit` label presence to detect hand-back, any change to the hand-back contract (label name, comment format, or marker location) in this ticket would require updating `land` as well.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how S5's emergency-stop sets `phase=paused` (e.g., a `pause()` / `resume()` method on the state machine, a shared flag file, or a signal) and enumerate that surface so S5 can implement against it without retrofitting loop.py.
findings.finding: The `paused` phase is defined as 'S5 emergency-stop', meaning S5 code will write or transition `wipChain.phase` to `paused`. S5 (`perpetual-ultrasonic-raccoon`) is not listed as a consumer or caller in the Files section. If S5 directly manipulates the `wipChain` object or calls a state-transition method in `loop.py`, it is an unenumerated external caller.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the 'Why' section (or a brief 'Approach' note) stating the positive rationale for the in-memory FSM: single-instance process-scoped state is sufficient because crash recovery is handled by re-selecting from Gerrit on restart (stateless re-hydration), and HA/persistent state is explicitly deferred to a future epic.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section explains why the plan *exists* (serialization + safety of the loop) but does not state a positive rationale for WHY an in-memory state machine was chosen as the implementation mechanism — e.g., why not a persistent FSM backed by the state volume, or a simpler imperative loop with inline re-check. The single-instance / HA-deferred rationale lives in S2/S5 but not in this plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan (e.g., under 'Why' or a new one-line 'Approach' note): 'In-memory state is intentional: on crash/restart the bot re-hydrates by re-querying Gerrit (open Autosubmit+submittable changes), so no persistent phase store is needed. Persistent HA state is deferred to a future epic (S5 design context).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
